rules.condition: [Patch rule — basal_cell_carcinoma_vs_benign_keratosis] Lesion is small and discretely bordered, surrounded by normal hair-bearing skin with multiple terminal hairs traversing the field; Central pigmentation consists of loosely aggregated brown-to-dark-brown dots and globules without uniform nesting or radial streaming; Peripheral lesion border fades irregularly into surrounding pinkish skin (moth-eaten edge) rather than forming a sharp, rolled, or translucent margin; Background skin shows diffuse pinkish-erythematous hue consistent with superficial vascular pattern, not arborizing telangiectatic vessels; No classic BCC structures present: no arborizing/branching vessels, no blue-gray ovoid nests, no leaf-like areas, no spoke-wheel or concentric structures; No ulceration or central erosion visible within the pigmented zone
rules.action: Classify as Benign Keratosis. Confidence: medium. Rule: When a small, centrally-located pigmented lesion on hair-bearing skin shows a loosely clustered arrangement of dark-brown to black globules and dots with a moth-eaten or irregular peripheral fading pattern, set against a diffuse pinkish-erythematous background with visible follicular openings and terminal hairs, and lacks arborizing vessels, leaf-like areas, or spoke-wheel structures, classify as Benign Keratosis.
